title: celtis hasseltii
intro: Celtis hasseltii, also known as the Hasselt's nettle tree or Indian hackberry, is a species of deciduous tree native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The extracts of Celtis hasseltii have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Celtis hasseltii are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
